rulings.Case Title: JOHN GIDDING et al VS. IVAN KAYE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Quash Subpoena Duces Tecum On Alan Pliska Or In The Alternative To Limit The Scope Of Records Sought Under A Protective Order
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, October 22, 2014, Line 1, PLAINTIFF JOHN GIDDING's Mtn To Quash Subpoena Duces Tecum On Alan Pliska Or In The Alternative To Limit The Scope Of Records Sought Under A Protective Order Pro Tem Judge Bruce Highman, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion to quash is granted in part. The subpoena is a trial subpoena for business records. The court, exercising discretion, believes that a trial business records subpoena generally should not make broad requests for all documents related to this or that, but rather should only request specific documents. Accordingly, the subpoena is quashed as to document requests A, B, E-J, and M-O. The motion to quash is denied as to requests C and D. The documents to be produced at the hearing in November in response to these requests will be subject to a protective order. A hearing is required to discuss the protective order. The motion to quash is granted as to K and L without prejudice to requesting the financial statements through a subpoena which requires the accountant or another qualified witness to testify live as to the preparation of the financial statements. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to bruce.highman@highmanlaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/JPT

rulings.Case Title: GANADERIA BRASIL INDUSTRIA E COMERCIO DE ACESSORIO VS. LEVI STRAUSS & CO. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Levi Strauss & Co. And Defendant Maryann Nichol'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration, Or In The Alternative Stay Litigation Pending Arbitration
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, October 22, 2014, Line 11, DEFENDANT LEVI STRAUSS & CO., MARYANN NICHOLS' Motion To Compel Arbitration Or, In The Alternative, Stay Litigation Pending Arbitration. Granted. Although Defendants are not signatories to the Trademark Licensing Agreement containing the arbitration provision, equitable estoppel applies. "[T]he equitable estoppel doctrine applies when a party has signed an agreement to arbitrate but attempts to avoid arbitration by suing nonsignatory defendants for claims that are based on the same facts and are inherently inseparable from arbitrable claims against signatory defendants." (Metalclad Corp. v. Ventana Environmental Org. Partnership (2003) 109 Cal.App.4th 1705, 1713.) The facts alleged in the Second Amended Complaint against Defendants are inherently inseparable from the arbitrable claims against Levi Spain, the signatory to the Trademark Licensing Agreement. Where it is alleged that the parent company caused its subsidiary to violate an agreement, the conduct complained of against the parent company is "intimately founded in and intertwined with the underlying¿contract." (Id. at 1717.) Plaintiff has not established that Defendant waived its right to compel arbitration. All six causes of action in the Second Amended Complaint shall be arbitrated, in light of the fact that Plaintiff agrees to send the fraud claim to arbitration if the Court orders arbitration of the other claims. (See Plaintiff's Corrected Opposition, p. 2, footnote 2.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: MICHAEL MORAN VS. YELLOW CAB COOPERATIVE, INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn For Protective Order
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, October 22, 2014, Line 8, PLAINTIFF MICHAEL MORAN's motion For Protective Order Pro Tem Judge Bruce Highman, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. Sanctions are denied. The court is tentatively inclined to allow the discovery subject to a protective order other than discovery of tax returns, filings with the Equalization Board, attorney-client communications, and confidential documents written by plaintiff's attorneys or persons hired by the attorneys that qualify as work product. The court tentatively believes that customer names, other than of customers who allegedly cancelled because of the subject incident, should be redacted. Names of other owners perhaps should be redacted from documents showing how much they received. Other redactions may be needed and should be discussed at the hearing. The court is interested in knowing if defendant is going to show the documents to an accounting expert. The court wants to discuss the protective order. Also the date for serving responses and documents. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to bruce.highman@highmanlaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/jpt

rulings.Case Title: PRECISION INDUSTRIAL CONSTRUCTORS, INC. VS. SNOWFLAKE MILL INVESTORS, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Production, And From Interrogatories
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, October 22, 2014, Line 9, PLAINTIFF PRECISION INDUSTRIAL CONSTRUCTORS, INC's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Requests For Production, And Form Interrogatories Pro Tem Judge Bruce Highman, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required to discuss a protective order. As to all requests at issue in the motion, where PIC said it would produce documents related to the FAC, it shall instead produce them related to the SAC. The responses to all requests for which the court is ordering additional document production shall be amended and re-verified to reflect the additional documents being produced. All documents and responses to be produced by 11-12. Notwithstanding what is ordered to be produced below as to specific requests, the following need not be produced-- tax returns, attorney-client communications, and confidential documents written by the attorneys or persons hired by them that qualify as work product; and any confidential financial information of any nonparty may be redacted and need not be produced. This is without prejudice to plaintiff hereafter seeking such financial information after providing notice to the nonparty and showing good cause sufficient to overcome the privacy rights of the nonparty. 40 and 41. Granted in part. Defendants shall also produce all documents responsive to these requests that are related to: (1) work involving hazardous materials that was performed by any contractor of defendants at the Facility; (2) the release of any hazardous materials at the Facility that reasonably might have been caused by any contractor of defendants. Defendants shall also produce documents sufficient to identify the name, address, and phone number of each contractor they have hired to work at the facility. 55, 56, 59, 62, 63, 64, 70, 87, 3.7. Granted. 57 and 60. Granted in part. Defendants shall also produce all documents responsive to these requests that are related to any of the assets that plaintiff had purchased. 73 and 74. Hearing required. How many members and shareholders are there? Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to bruce.highman@highmanlaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. rulings.Case Title: CRAIG K MARTIN VS. DOES 1-50, INCLUSIVE
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, October 22, 2014, Line 12, DEFENDANT RICHARD SZETO'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendant's Demurrer is sustained without leave to amend. The Complaint fails to allege any conduct by Defendant to support any of the causes of action asserted against him. The Complaint also fails to allege any facts to support the formation or existence of a conspiracy between Defendant and any other named Defendant. Further, Plaintiff bases Defendant's liability as a coconspirator on an erroneous legal basis-i.e., that Defendant became a member of the alleged conspiracy by and through his involvement in or status as a party to an action filed against him. Thus, the Complaint cannot be cured by amendment because such conduct could not form nor further any such alleged conspiracy. Moreover, with respect to any claims resulting from alleged violations of the Ralph Act (Civ. Code § 51.7) or the Bane Act (Civ. Code § 52.1), Plaintiff fails to allege that any violent acts or threatened violent acts were committed by anyone, let alone the moving party. Plaintiff alleges verbal abuse by another name defendant but this is not sufficient to state a constitutional deprivation of rights per Title 42 U.S.C. § 1983. (Oltarzewski v. Ruggiero (9th Cir. 1987) 830 F.2d 136, 139. Nor are any facts alleged linking the moving party to this conduct other than the bare conclusion that he is a coconspirator. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if in pro per on the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: CRAIG K MARTIN VS. DOES 1-50, INCLUSIVE
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, October 22, 2014, Line 12, DEFENDANT RICHARD SZETO's SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE. Defendant's Special Motion to Strike is denied. Defendant has not met his burden of showing that the causes of action asserted against him arise from protected activity. Defendant's contention that his "involvement" in or status as a party to an action filed against him constitutes protected speech on a public matter is supported neither by coherent argument nor applicable authority. rulings.Case Title: JAMES MONTOYA VS. UNIVERSITY OF PACIFIC, SCHOOL OF DENTRISTY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, October 22, 2014, Line 7, DEFENDANTS UNIVERSITY OF THE PACIFIC, ARTHUR A DUGONI SCHOOL's MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Denied. Although Plaintiff filed its First Amended Complaint beyond the 10 day period to amend, Defendants have not established prejudice by the late-filing. No trial date is set and discovery is in its early stages. Further, although Plaintiff added a cause of action beyond the scope of the permitted amendment, the Court declines to strike the cause of action for judicial economy reasons. If Plaintiff were to file a motion to add the cause of action, it would almost certainly be granted, given the case is still at the early stages and California's liberal policy regarding amendments. Defendants' request for fees and costs is denied. Defendants' moving papers do not address what costs will actually be incurred in filing an amended answer and supplemental discovery responses. Plaintiff's request for fees for opposing this motion is denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. rulings.Case Title: SERGEY KHOLOPOV et al VS. IRINA LITVAK et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, October 22, 2014, Line 8, CROSS DEFENDANTS SERGEY KHOLOPOV, SIARHEI AHANESIAN's DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT. The Cross-Defendants' Demurrer to the First Cause of Action for Fraudulent Concealment is sustained without leave to amend pursuant to C.C.P. § 430.10(e). To plead a cause of action for fraudulent concealment, the Cross-Complainants must allege that the Cross-Defendants were in a fiduciary relationship with the Cross-Complainants. Roddenberry v. Roddenberry (1996) 44 Cal.App.4th 634, 665-66; Restatment.3d, Agency § 8.01. It is undisputed that Cross-Defendants were employees of the Cross-Complainants and employees do not have a fiduciary relationship with their employers. See Calvao v. Super. Ct. (1988) 201 Cal.App.3d 921, 923 (holding there is no confidential or fiduciary relationship in an employment contract context). Cross-Complainants fail to show in what manner they can amend the Cross-Complaint to cure the defect. Goodman v. Kennedy (1976) 18 Cal.3d 335, 349. Furthermore, a fraudulent concealment cause of action must be pled with specificity including who concealed what information from whom, when the alleged concealments occurred, and how the concealments occurred. See Stansfield v. Starkey (1990) 220 Cal.App.3d 59, 73-74. The Demurrer to the Second Cause of Action for Conspiracy to Defraud is sustained without leave to amend pursuant to C.C.P. § 430.10(e). The Cross-Complaint fails to satisfy the requirement that a conspiracy cause of action be grounded in an underlying tort. See Applied Equip. Corp. v. Litton Saudi Arabia Ltd. (1994) 7 Cal.4th 503, 510-11. The Demurrer to the Third Cause of Action for Breach of Contract is sustained with 20 days leave to amend pursuant to C.C.P. § 430.10(e). The Cross-Complaint must allege the specific parties to the employment contract and the terms of the employment contract the Cross-Defendants allegedly violated in addition to the 15% service charge policy. See, e.g., Construction Protective Svcs. v. TIG Specialty Ins. Co. (2002) 29 Cal.4th 189, 199; see also, Careau & Co. v. Security Pacific Business Credit, Inc. (1990) 222 Cal.App.3d 1371, 1388. This cause of action must also comply with C.R.C. 2.112 and state which party is asserting the cause of action since more than one party is represented on the pleadings. C.R.C. 2.112. The Demurrer to the Fourth Cause of Action for Unjust Enrichment is sustained without leave to amend pursuant to C.C.P. § 430.10(e). Unjust enrichment is not a valid cause of action but rather a "basis for obtaining restitution" and this cause of action fails as a matter of law. See McKell v. Washington Mutual, Inc. (2006) 142 Cal.App.4th 1457, 1490; see also McBride v. Boughton (2004) 123 Cal.App.4th 379, 387. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)
